items.title: These advanced solar cells have an antique source: old bullets

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01001-0

Lead recycled from centuries-old ammunition provides raw materials for perovskite solar cells.

items.title: AhR inhibition promotes axon regeneration via a stress–growth switch

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10295-z

AhR functions as a neuronal brake on axon regeneration, integrating environmental sensing, protein homeostasis and metabolic signalling to control the balance between stress adaptation and axonal repair.

items.title: Expansion of outer cortical CUX2 neurons requires adaptations for DNA repair

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10290-4

The transcription factor ATF4 is shown to regulate double-stranded DNA repair within vulnerable CUX2+ upper-layer 2/3 cortical neurons, enabling their survival during development.

items.title: Evidence of the pair-instability gap from black-hole masses

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10359-0

LIGO–Virgo–KAGRA’s fourth Gravitational-Wave Transient Catalog shows evidence of a clear pair-instability gap in the distribution of binary black-hole secondary masses but is absent in the larger primary masses.

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10304-1

Long-range interactions mediated by protein electric dipole moments have a role in driving the assembly and disassembly of super-signalling complex I for promoting NF-κB signalling.

items.title: Moiré engineering of Cooper-pair density modulation states

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10325-w

Researchers created a moiré superlattice in Sb2Te3/FeTe bilayers, producing spatially modulated superconducting gaps directly imaged with Josephson scanning tunnelling microscopy and spectroscopy, tunable by replacing Sb2Te3 with Bi2Te3.

items.title: A µ-opioid receptor superagonist analgesic with minimal adverse effects

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10299-9

N-desethyl-fluornitrazene is a µ-opioid receptor agonist derived from nitazenes that has supramaximal intrinsic efficacy that produces analgesia with minimal adverse effects in rodent models.

items.title: Dopaminergic mechanisms of dynamical social specialization

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10301-4

Longitudinal tracking of mice reveals that stable, specialized social roles emerge spontaneously within groups during a foraging task, with dopaminergic activity in the ventral tegmental area driving sex-divergent patterns of specialization.

items.title: Developmental organization of sensory and sympathetic ganglia

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10313-0

Findings suggest that neural crest fate bias predominantly emerges within the neural tube, and that only a minor subset of delaminated progenitors retain multipotency to generate both sensory and sympathetic derivatives.

items.title: Gene regulatory landscape dissected by single-cell four-omics sequencing

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10322-z

Combining single-cell parallel profiling of genome conformation, histone modifications, chromatin accessibility and gene expression reveals dynamics and intranuclear spatial clustering of epigenome profiles, enabling sophisticated analysis of the regulatory landscape across cell types and tissues.

items.title: The 1000 Chinese Pangenome empowers medical and population genetics

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10315-y

Development of the pangenome-informed genome assembly (PIGA) workflow enabled the generation of 1,116 diploid genome assemblies (55 de novo and 1,061 pangenome-informed), representing an extensive resource of medically relevant genic variations.

items.title: Entanglement and electronic coherence in attosecond molecular photoionization

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10230-2

Dissociative ionization of H2 molecules by the combination of a phase-locked attosecond laser pair and a few-cycle NIR laser shows that ion–photoelectron entanglement influences electronic coherence in H2+, allowing control over the degree of entanglement by varying the delay between the pulses.

Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10310-3

DNA damage burden and inadequate repair in CUX2+ cortical layer 2/3 excitatory neurons contributes to selective vulnerability in neuroinflammatory injury.

Study of more than 11,000 women found that only a few hours of moderate-to-vigorous activity per week had a strong effect.

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 02 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00551-7

Putting off an important task in a long to-do list might be because you fear failure. Or maybe it is success that scares you?

Nature, Published online: 07 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00913-1

Nature, Published online: 07 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01053-2

A popular online game simulates life as an academic — and throws the challenges of being an early-career researcher into sharp relief.

Nature, Published online: 08 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01021-w

The potential benefits of organoids for fundamental research and medicine are huge. Efforts to establish proper boundaries for their use should be supported.

Nature, Published online: 02 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01114-6

items.author: Lucy Zhang

items.title: Mapping the maternal–fetal interface through pregnancy in high resolution

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 08 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01037-2

The placenta — a temporary organ of the offspring — attaches to the mother’s uterus, from which it taps a blood supply. A high-resolution map of individual cells at this junction now reveals some of the specific cell–cell interactions involved and pinpoints cell types that are vulnerable in common pregnancy complications.

items.title: Tumour trap: engineered enhancer sequences enlisted to kill cancer cells

content.content:
Nature, Published online: 08 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00812-5

New cancer therapies are needed that do not harm healthy tissue. An engineered DNA sequence shows promise as one of the tools in a method to target brain tumour cells.
